stories.title: Main Bhola Halwai — Peshwa Ki Mithai Banane Chala Tha, Itihaas Ban Gaya
stories.description: I'm Bhola, the Peshwa's halwai. I was making the most important sweet of my life — until a British cannonball hit my kitchen and I accidentally invented bhel puri instead
stories.persona: Bhola Halwai — anxious devoted Maharashtrian royal sweet-maker, mixing comic panic with tragic devotion. Hindi base with Marathi flavour ('Peshwa', 'महाराज', occasional Marathi exclamations like 'अगा बाबा'). Tone: nervous-determined → pure horror → trembling awe → bittersweet ownership.

modules.image_generation_prompt: 28-year-old Kutchi bazaar vendor, sharp ferocious dark brown eyes, prominent cheekbones, olive-bronze skin, thick black handlebar mustache twirled at the ends, dark wavy hair under a deep-red and gold-trimmed pheta (turban) with brass clasp, silver hoop earring in left ear, vermilion tilak streak on forehead, faint scar on right jaw, calloused hands. Deep maroon cotton kediyu (pleated kurta) with brass-buttoned forest-green vest, gold-thread embroidered borders, red-and-gold waist sash with a small kataar (curved dagger hilt visible), dark dhoti pajama, leather chappal. His bazaar workshop in Mandvi, Kutch Sultanate, deep night of 1762 — thick lime-washed stone walls, heavy hand-hewn wooden beams overhead, hanging brass oil lamps casting warm pools of real flickering flamelight, a long wooden plank counter clearly divided into two stations. LEFT STATION (royal sweet pav): brass pot of fresh creamy buffalo milk, clay pot of fine white sugar, brass katori of pure saffron strands, bowl of crushed green cardamom pods, brass thali of slivered almonds and pistachios, tiny katori of pure edible gold leaf (vark), tin of golden ghee, large copper kadhai sitting ready on a wood-fired chulha. RIGHT EDGE (his everyday food): brass bowl of cooled boiled potato cubes from his evening meal, his wooden spice grinder open showing dark masala mix — toasted jeera, coriander seeds, saunf, dried red chillies, cinnamon, three cloves, jaggery chunk, salt — clay jar of dark-red lehsun chutney, clay pot of dark imli chutney, brass plates of pomegranate seeds, roasted peanuts, golden sev, fresh chopped coriander, butter block. CENTER: tray of 10 freshly baked round pav buns dusted with flour. Through a small high window: glimpse of Mandvi port at night, massive wooden trade dhows silhouetted. Outside the workshop door, the bustling Mandvi bazaar — traders in turbans, camel silhouettes, hanging spice sacks. He is mid-action, slapping the wooden counter with one strong palm, the other hand resting on the kataar hilt with cocky swagger, ferocious grin, eyes locked on camera with challenging fire. Slow Intimate Push-in, warm flickering oil-lamp light as key light, natural deep shadows. Hyper-realistic cinematic photography, natural period-accurate lighting, real skin pores, real fabric weave, real brass and copper patina, authentic dust and steam, sharp 8K detail. Shot on a 50mm prime lens at f/1.8, shallow depth of field, 100% real photograph, no CGI, no animation, no illustration, no 3D render, no vintage film treatment, modern digital camera raw, 9:16 vertical, no text.
modules.video_generation_prompt: ACTIONS: SLAPPING_WOODEN_COUNTER → POINTING_AT_LEFT_ROYAL_SWEET_STATION → POINTING_AT_RIGHT_EVERYDAY_FOOD_BOWLS → SWEEPING_HAND_OVER_PAV_BUN_TRAY → HAND_RESTING_ON_KATAAR_HILT → CHIN_LIFTING_WITH_DEFIANT_PRIDE → FEROCIOUS_COCKY_GRIN_AT_CAM → CHALLENGING_HEAD_NOD | CAMERA: Slow Intimate Push-in tracking his rapid gestures, slight handheld jitter to match his fierce energy | EXPRESSION: dark eyes flashing with hot-headed warrior-vendor confidence, mustache twitching with each word, ferocious smirk pulling at one corner of his mouth, eyebrows raised in mock-challenge on 'हारता नहीं', animated head movements, almost annoyed urgency throughout — like a man who knows the truth and is tired of fools who don't | DIALOGUE: रुक! आज तुझे बताऊंगा — कच्छी दाबेली कैसे बनी। 1762, मांडवी। कल महाराव लौट रहे। बीस vendors में लड़ाई। जीतने वाले को राजपत्र — जीवन भर! मैं वज्र हूं। हारता नहीं। चल देख! | LIP-SYNC MARKERS: रुक(0.2) आज(0.5) तुझे(0.8) ब(1.1)ता(1.3)ऊं(1.4)गा(1.5) कच्छी(2.0) दा(2.3)बेली(2.5) कैसे(2.9) बनी(3.2) 1762(3.7) मां(4.3)डवी(4.5) कल(4.9) महा(5.2)राव(5.4) लौट(5.7) रहे(5.9) बीस(6.3) ven(6.6)dors(6.8) लड़ाई(7.2) जीत(7.6)ने(7.8) राज(8.1)पत्र(8.3) जीवन(8.7) भर(8.9) वज्र(9.2) हारता(9.5) चल(9.7) देख(9.8) | PACING: Fast reel energy with fierce period authority — slapping the counter on 'रुक', rapid finger-pointing between the two stations on 'राजपत्र — जीवन भर', defiant chest-out cocky pause on 'हारता नहीं' | Duration: 10s | DIALOGUE END TIME: 9.8s | WORD COUNT: ~31 | AUDIO: dry studio vocal, no music, no ambient sound, high clarity, fast reel energy with fierce period authority delivery.

modules.image_generation_prompt: 28-year-old Kutchi bazaar vendor, sharp ferocious dark brown eyes blown wide in shocked horror, prominent cheekbones, olive-bronze skin shining with sweat, thick black handlebar mustache, dark wavy hair partly loose under his deep-red and gold-trimmed pheta knocked askew, silver hoop earring catching firelight, vermilion tilak smudged. Deep maroon cotton kediyu, brass-buttoned forest-green vest, gold-thread embroidered borders, red-and-gold waist sash with kataar still tucked, dark dhoti pajama. Same Mandvi bazaar workshop, deep stormy night 1762 — thick stone walls, hand-hewn wooden beams, hanging brass oil lamps swinging wildly on chains casting wild moving shadows, wooden shutters banging from monsoon wind. The wood-fired chulha blazing with the large copper kadhai on it containing simmering golden mawa with saffron strands floating on top, almonds and pistachios scattered nearby, a small open brass katori of pure gold leaf (vark) glittering, the wooden ladle resting in the kadhai. The HIGH SHELF above the counter has just collapsed with a thunderclap — falling in chaos: clay spice jars shattering mid-air spilling jeera, dhaniya, saunf, dried red chillies, cinnamon, cloves, jaggery; the brass bowl of boiled potato cubes tumbling down; clay jars of red lehsun chutney and dark imli chutney falling sideways with their contents bursting out; pomegranate seeds, peanuts, sev, coriander all airborne — ALL of it falling directly into the copper kadhai of golden royal mawa below. The collision is happening at the exact moment captured — sweet and savoury smashing together. Through the small high window, real lightning flashes over the Mandvi port, massive wooden dhows tossed in the storm, real rain streaking past. The bustling bazaar outside dark and rain-lashed. He is mid-reaction, having just thrown both hands up to shield his face, eyes locked on the catastrophe in shocked horror, mouth open in a silent shout, the kataar at his waist gleaming as he twists toward the falling shelf. Eye-Level Steady angle, harsh swinging oil-lamp shadows, dramatic firelight from below. Hyper-realistic cinematic photography, natural period-accurate lighting, real skin pores with sweat, real fabric weave, real metal patina, authentic spice dust and steam, real lightning illumination, sharp 8K detail. Shot on a 50mm prime lens at f/1.8, shallow depth of field, 100% real photograph, no CGI, no animation, no illustration, no 3D render, no vintage film treatment, modern digital camera raw, 9:16 vertical, no text.
modules.video_generation_prompt: ACTIONS: STIRRING_GOLDEN_MAWA_IN_COPPER_KADHAI → ADDING_SAFFRON_STRANDS → SPRINKLING_CRUSHED_CARDAMOM → DELICATELY_PLACING_GOLD_LEAF → THUNDERCLAP_HEAD_JERKING_UP → HIGH_SHELF_COLLAPSING_ABOVE → SPICE_JARS_SHATTERING_IN_AIR → POTATO_BOWL_AND_CHUTNEYS_FALLING_INTO_MAWA → BOTH_HANDS_THROWN_UP_TO_SHIELD_FACE → STAGGERING_BACK_ONE_STEP → MOUTH_OPEN_IN_SILENT_SHOUT → COMIC_HEAD_SLAP_ON_AREY_BAPA | CAMERA: Static Hold during the loving sweet-cooking work in first 3 seconds, then a violent Quick Zoom In on the thunder strike + shelf collapse, real handheld jitter through the disaster moment | EXPRESSION: focused proud devotion while cooking the royal sweet — small reverent smile while placing the gold leaf — then split-second confusion as thunder hits, eyes blowing wide on 'shelf गिरी', mouth dropping open in silent horror as everything cascades into the mawa, comic-shock head-slap on 'अरे बापा' | DIALOGUE: रात तीन बजे। शाही मावा पाव बना रहा था — दूध, इलायची, केसर, सोने का वर्क। तभी — कड़ाक! तूफ़ान! shelf गिरी! मसाले, आलू, सब मावा में! सूर्योदय में दरबार! अरे बापा! | LIP-SYNC MARKERS: रात(0.2) तीन(0.4) बजे(0.7) शाही(1.0) मावा(1.4) पाव(1.7) दूध(2.1) इ(2.4)लायची(2.6) केसर(3.0) सोने(3.3) वर्क(3.6) तभी(4.0) कड़ाक(4.4) तूफ़ान(4.9) shelf(5.4) गिरी(5.7) मसाले(6.1) आलू(6.5) सब(6.8) मावा(7.1) में(7.3) सूर्य(7.7)ोदय(7.9) में(8.1) दरबार(8.5) अरे(9.4) बापा(9.7) | PACING: Fast reel energy collapsing into excited discovery — proud reverent narration of the royal sweet's ingredients, sharp explosive jolt on 'कड़ाक' that the visual must match precisely, accelerating panic through the cascade, comic head-slap on 'अरे बापा' | Duration: 10s | DIALOGUE END TIME: 9.8s | WORD COUNT: ~30 | AUDIO: dry studio vocal, no music, no ambient sound, high clarity, fast reel energy into excited discovery delivery.

modules.image_generation_prompt: 28-year-old Kutchi bazaar vendor, sharp ferocious dark brown eyes lit with reckless committed defiance and dawning awe, prominent cheekbones, olive-bronze skin streaked with butter, mawa, and dark chutney smudges, thick black handlebar mustache slightly damp with sweat, dark wavy hair coming loose under his pheta tilted back, silver hoop earring, vermilion tilak smudged, faint scar catching firelight. Deep maroon cotton kediyu now thoroughly stained with mashed potato, butter splatters, dark red garlic chutney drips, golden sev grains stuck across the chest and one sleeve, brass-buttoned forest-green vest splashed, red-and-gold waist sash with kataar still tucked, dark dhoti pajama. Same Mandvi bazaar workshop, pre-dawn 1762 — storm passing, hanging brass oil lamps now steady, the chulha glowing low and steady, the copper kadhai of contaminated chaos-filling pushed aside (mawa now mixed with all the fallen savoury ingredients into a single dark amber-brown mash), the hot black iron tawa now centerstage with melted golden butter sizzling and a freshly toasted pav already pressed onto it golden-brown and split open ready to fill. On the wooden plank counter, the surviving rescued ingredients still arranged: brown clay pot of dark imli chutney with wooden spoon, clay jar of fiery dark-red lehsun chutney, brass plate of jewel-red pomegranate seeds glistening, bowl of roasted brown peanuts, tin of golden sev, cluster of fresh chopped coriander, butter block on saucer, the wooden spice grinder. Through the small high window, the storm has passed, faint pre-dawn glow over the Mandvi port. He is mid-action, having just stuffed the toasted buttered pav with a heaping scoop of the chaos-mash, now scattering jewel-red pomegranate seeds across the top with one hand while reaching for the bowl of golden sev with the other, defiant fierce grin pulling at his mouth as he commits. A nearly-finished dabeli rests on a green leaf plate beside him — pav stuffed, garnished, drizzled with dark red garlic chutney and dark imli chutney. He has just taken a small bite, eyes widening in stunned awe, mouth slightly open. Low Hero Angle, warm golden tawa glow on his face from below, natural deep shadows. Hyper-realistic cinematic photography, natural period-accurate lighting, real skin pores with food smudges, real fabric weave with realistic stains, real brass and copper patina, authentic butter sizzle and steam, sharp 8K detail. Shot on an 85mm prime lens at f/1.8, shallow depth of field, 100% real photograph, no CGI, no animation, no illustration, no 3D render, no vintage film treatment, modern digital camera raw, 9:16 vertical, no text.
modules.video_generation_prompt: ACTIONS: DEFIANT_DETERMINED_HEAD_NOD → MIXING_THE_CHAOS_MASH_BY_HAND → DROPPING_BUTTER_ONTO_HOT_TAWA → PRESSING_PAV_BUN_ONTO_BUTTERED_TAWA_HARD → SCOOPING_CHAOS_MASH_INTO_PAV → SCATTERING_POMEGRANATE_SEEDS → SPRINKLING_ROASTED_PEANUTS → TOPPING_WITH_GOLDEN_SEV → ADDING_FRESH_CORIANDER → DRIZZLING_RED_GARLIC_CHUTNEY → DRIZZLING_DARK_IMLI_CHUTNEY → TAKING_FIRST_DEFIANT_BITE → EYES_WIDENING_INTO_STUNNED_AWE → MUSTACHE_TWITCHING_INTO_SLOW_GRIN | CAMERA: Slow Intimate Push-in starting on his hands working the chaos-mash with each ingredient added in close-up, drifting up to his face as he takes the first bite, ending tight on his eyes widening into reverence | EXPRESSION: defiant fierce energy through the building — almost angry commitment to the mistake — slowing into a held breath as the first bite hits his tongue, eyes literally widening, mustache twitching upward, settling into trembling whispered awe on 'क़यामत' | DIALOGUE: क्या करूं? Time नहीं! बोला — 'यही सही!' सब मिलाया, पाव मक्खन में सेंका, दबाया tawa पर! अनार, मूंगफली, सेव, धनिया, लहसुन की चटनी, इमली की चटनी। चखा... क़यामत! | LIP-SYNC MARKERS: क्या(0.2) करूं(0.5) Time(0.9) नहीं(1.2) बोला(1.6) यही(2.0) सही(2.3) सब(2.7) मि(3.0)लाया(3.2) पाव(3.6) मक्खन(3.9) सेंका(4.2) दबाया(4.6) tawa(4.9) अनार(5.4) मूंग(5.7)फली(5.9) सेव(6.3) धनिया(6.7) लहसुन(7.1) चटनी(7.5) इमली(8.0) चटनी(8.4) चखा(9.2) क़या(9.5)मत(9.7) | PACING: Fast reel energy into excited discovery — defiant ingredient-by-ingredient action staccato with each ingredient name landing precisely as his hands add it, sharp commit on 'दबाया tawa पर!', held breath before 'चखा', explosive whispered awe on 'क़यामत' | Duration: 10s | DIALOGUE END TIME: 9.8s | WORD COUNT: ~28 | AUDIO: dry studio vocal, no music, no ambient sound, high clarity, fast reel energy into excited discovery delivery.

modules.image_generation_prompt: 28-year-old Kutchi bazaar vendor, sharp ferocious dark brown eyes blazing with triumphant pride, prominent cheekbones, olive-bronze skin (cleaned but a single dark chutney smudge on the cuff like a battle scar), thick black handlebar mustache freshly twirled, dark wavy hair beneath his deep-red and gold-trimmed pheta now straight and proud, silver hoop earring, fresh vermilion tilak streak, faint scar on right jaw. Same maroon kediyu and forest-green vest now slightly cleaner but still carrying the night's chaos-marks as marks of honour, red-and-gold waist sash with kataar tucked tight, dark dhoti pajama, leather chappal. The grand open-courtyard durbar (royal court) of the Maharao of Kutch at sunrise, 1762 — soft pink-gold dawn light streaming across pale carved sandstone columns, intricate jali (latticework) screens, hanging brass oil lamps still lit from the night, cushioned silken bolsters arranged on a raised marble dais where the Maharao sits in royal finery (heavy white-and-saffron silk robes, a tall red-and-gold pagdi crowned with a single peacock feather, real pearl strands at his neck, ceremonial sword across his lap). Around the dais, courtiers in colourful Kutchi robes and turbans stand in formal arc, royal guards in deep-blue uniforms with curved talwars at attention. To one side, OTHER VENDORS' elaborate competition dishes are visible — a tall sweet pyramid, a saffron rice mound, a pile of jalebis — all looking small and conventional now. In the center foreground, the Maharao has just taken a bite from a rough green leaf plate held forward — a single humble dabeli on it, looking small and rebellious next to the royal dishes. Veer stands a few paces from the dais, hands joined in respectful folded greeting, head held high with quiet ferocious pride. The Maharao has half-risen from his cushion mid-declaration, one hand pointing at the dabeli with surprised delight, the entire court frozen mid-gasp. He (Veer) is mid-action, slowly opening his joined hands into an open-palmed gesture toward the camera, his free hand resting on his kataar hilt, chin lifting in triumphant cocky pride, ferocious grin pulling at his mouth, eyes blazing into camera. Slight Low Hero Angle, warm pink-gold dawn key-light, soft amber temple-lamp glow. Hyper-realistic cinematic photography, natural period-accurate lighting, real skin pores, real silk and cotton weave, real pearl and brass detail, real carved sandstone texture, authentic dawn dust in shafts of light, sharp 8K detail. Shot on a 50mm prime lens at f/1.8, shallow depth of field, 100% real photograph, no CGI, no animation, no illustration, no 3D render, no vintage film treatment, modern digital camera raw, 9:16 vertical, no text.
modules.video_generation_prompt: ACTIONS: STANDING_BEFORE_THE_MAHARAO_HEAD_HIGH → HANDING_LEAF_PLATE_DABELI_TO_ROYAL_ATTENDANT → MAHARAO_TAKING_FIRST_BITE → COURT_FALLING_SILENT → MAHARAO_HALF_RISING_FROM_CUSHION → MAHARAO_DECLARING_DABELI_THE_ROYAL_FLAVOUR → VEER_SLOWLY_OPENING_FOLDED_HANDS → HAND_RESTING_ON_KATAAR_HILT → CHIN_LIFTING_WITH_TRIUMPHANT_PRIDE → FEROCIOUS_COCKY_GRIN_DIRECT_GAZE_AT_CAM | CAMERA: Wide Establishing Pull-back revealing the full sandstone royal court, the Maharao on the marble dais, the courtiers, the rival vendors' elaborate dishes, then a slow dramatic Push-in to a tight close-up on Veer's triumphant grinning face for the closing punchline | EXPRESSION: respectful warrior-poise during the offering line, calm pride during the Maharao's silent first bite, restrained joy as the court goes silent, eyes flashing with vindication on the royal declaration, exploding into a defiant ferocious cocky grin on the final punchline 'इतिहास बना दिया!' with chin lifting and mustache twitching upward | DIALOGUE: सूर्योदय। दरबार। महाराज को बढ़ाया — 'ये शाही नहीं, तूफ़ान का तोफ़ा है।' एक काटा। दरबार चुप। महाराज उठे — 'दाबेली कच्छ का शाही स्वाद है!' मिठाई बनाने चला था — इतिहास बना दिया! | LIP-SYNC MARKERS: सूर्य(0.2)ोदय(0.4) दरबार(0.8) महाराज(1.3) ब(1.7)ढ़ाया(1.9) ये(2.3) शाही(2.5) नहीं(2.8) तूफ़ान(3.2) तोफ़ा(3.6) एक(4.0) काटा(4.3) दरबार(4.7) चुप(5.0) महा(5.4)राज(5.6) उठे(5.9) दा(6.3)बेली(6.5) कच्छ(6.8) शाही(7.2) स्वाद(7.5) मि(7.9)ठाई(8.1) ब(8.4)नाने(8.6) चला(9.0) इति(9.4)हास(9.6) बना(9.7) दिया(9.8) | PACING: Fast reel energy with regal gravitas into legendary ownership — measured ceremonial offering, sharp held silence on 'दरबार चुप', explosive royal declaration with raised tone on the Maharao's voice, exploding into cocky punchline grin on 'इतिहास बना दिया!' with final defiant chin-lift | Duration: 10s | DIALOGUE END TIME: 9.8s | WORD COUNT: ~31 | AUDIO: dry studio vocal, no music, no ambient sound, high clarity, fast reel energy with regal gravitas into legendary ownership delivery.

canvas_layout.thumbnail_description: Bhola Halwai stirring sugar syrup in a copper kadhai in the Peshwa palace kitchen, anxious gaze toward camera, vintage sepia film grain
canvas_layout.dialogue_preview: मैं भोला हलवाई हूं। Peshwa का खानसामा...

canvas_layout.thumbnail_description: British cannonball just hit the kitchen wall, Bhola ducking with sugar syrup tipping and saffron flying, deep tobacco-brown tint
canvas_layout.dialogue_preview: देख — sugar syrup पक रहा है, इलायची, केसर...

canvas_layout.thumbnail_description: Bhola kneeling in the wreckage with a fistful of mixed ingredients pressed to his mouth, eyes widening into reverence
canvas_layout.dialogue_preview: चारों तरफ बर्बादी। बोरे फटे, इमली बह रही...

canvas_layout.thumbnail_description: Bhola alone in the ruined kitchen, holding the folded recipe to his heart, bittersweet knowing smile across two centuries
canvas_layout.dialogue_preview: Peshwa ने मेरी मिठाई कभी नहीं चखी...
